description_long: This object is a rolled-up newspaper, titled 'THE SILVER HERALD', with a visible masthead in bold black letters on a white background. The front page features a large, colorful photograph of a sky with clouds, likely a sunrise or sunset, and several columns of printed text. The newspaper is tightly rolled, giving it a cylindrical shape, and the print is visible along the curved surface, with headlines and smaller articles wrapping around the roll. The paper appears to be standard newsprint, thin and slightly matte, with a smooth but not glossy finish. There are no visible racks, windows, or payment mechanisms, indicating this is a single newspaper rather than a full stand or kiosk. The object is lightweight and portable, suitable for being picked up or carried by hand.
description: A rolled-up newspaper titled 'THE SILVER HERALD' with a bold masthead, a large sky photograph, and visible printed text on white newsprint.
description_view_0: Front view showing the masthead 'THE SILVER HERALD', a large sky photo, and main headlines.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the rolled edge and a partial view of the sky photograph wrapping around the paper.
description_view_2: Rear side view with mostly printed text and the continuation of the sky image, less of the masthead visible.
description_view_3: Edge view showing the thickness of the rolled newspaper and a sliver of the masthead and photo.
